panels.all: The 702 and 565 MHz curves track broad multi-MHz decay rather than an isolated central wing; lower bands are noisy or nearly linear.

verdicts.notes: Consistent with low-lag and support failure; diagnostic only.

panels.all: The 777 MHz panel is dominated by a broad decline and the remaining panels are noise-dominated.
The 728 MHz peak has a broad pedestal and the 524 MHz fit follows a nearly linear envelope.
All four ACFs are finite; fitted curves follow compact central peaks and then settle near the observed baselines without obvious broad-envelope capture.

verdicts.notes: Legends are readable and agree with four resolved physical subbands.

panels.all: The 757 and 505 MHz selected curves follow broad monotonic structure across most of the plotted range.
The 766 and 631 MHz curves retain broad multi-MHz tails; the low-lag control is unstable.

verdicts.notes: Diagnostic only despite three nominally resolved subbands.

panels.all: The 740 MHz selected curve is effectively a broad plateau over the full lag range rather than a localized central feature.

verdicts.notes: Artifact controls pass numerically, but the required visual morphology does not.

panels.all: The 750 MHz fit follows a broad declining envelope while the other bands are largely noise-like.

panels.left: All six median recovered widths lie on the identity line across 0.02 to 3 MHz.
panels.right: All three injected two-scale adoption bars are 1.0 above the 0.95 guide; both null false-split bars are zero.

verdicts.notes: Axes and category labels are legible after regeneration.

panels.all: The two high-band curves follow broad decay and the two low bands are strongly noise-dominated.

verdicts.notes: Only one valid subband; off-pulse evidence is inconclusive.

panels.all: The 768 and 625 MHz panels show broad envelope capture with very large amplitudes; middle panels are noise-like.
The 740 MHz ACF has a structured broad pedestal and the 632 MHz curve follows a broad decay; lower bands are noise-like.
The 724 and 625 MHz panels show broad structure while two bands are dominated by oscillatory noise.
Only the 739 MHz band has a visible decay and it is broad; the other three are high-amplitude noise patterns.
The 728 MHz band shows broad pedestal structure and all other bands are noise-dominated.
The 759 MHz fit follows a broad decline and the 578 MHz ACF has strong non-Lorentzian multi-MHz curvature.
The 739 and 620 MHz panels contain broad structure; 640 MHz is strongly oscillatory and non-Lorentzian.

verdicts.notes: Off-pulse and low-lag pass, but subband support is zero.

panels.all: The 733 MHz selected curve tracks a broad decay; remaining bands are unresolved or nearly linear/noisy.
The 755 MHz panel is broad and the other bands are unresolved noisy traces.
Three selected curves follow broad multi-MHz envelopes rather than isolated central peaks; the fourth band is noisy.

verdicts.notes: The off-pulse null fails and amplitudes are flagged.

panels.all: All selected curves show broad decay, with the 622 MHz band especially non-Lorentzian at large lag.

verdicts.notes: Only two physical supported subbands and alpha is unphysical.

panels.all: The visible 754 MHz central peak is compact and reaches its baseline rapidly; other bands are correctly shown as unresolved/noisy rather than fitted broad structure.

verdicts.notes: Visual morphology matches, but low-lag and support controls keep the record diagnostic.

panels.all: The 630 MHz fitted peak is compact; the other panels remain visibly unresolved and are not promoted by the figure.

verdicts.notes: Off-pulse and support controls fail independently.

panels.all: All four curves retain broad multi-MHz decay or pedestals rather than isolated central wings.

verdicts.notes: Off-pulse evidence is inconclusive despite four nominally resolved fits.

panels.all: The 753 and 622 MHz panels have broad tails/pedestals; the middle two contain narrow peaks but do not rescue the full-record morphology.

science_disposition: All required figures were rendered and reviewed. Injection recovery matches. Only chromatica_hi has both a matching campaign figure and passing artifact controls; every other record remains diagnostic_only.
